problem.title: Unique Binary Search Trees II
problem.description: Given an integer n, return all the structurally unique BST's (binary search trees), which has exactly n nodes of unique values from 1 to n.
assumptions: Return all actual tree structures (not just count).

example.output: All 5 structurally unique BSTs with nodes 1,2,3
example.explanation: Generate all trees: for each root i, recursively generate all left trees from [1..i-1] and right from [i+1..n].

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Recursive [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: For each value i in [start, end], treat i as the root; all values in [start, i-1] form the left subtrees and [i+1, end] form the right subtrees. Every combination of a left subtree and a right subtree yields one unique BST rooted at i, so the function returns a list that grows as all these combinations are joined. The key insight is that the BST property uniquely partitions the remaining values into the left and right subtrees once the root is chosen.
